title: panic: error opening storage.lock
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

While testing a new, freshly built Ubuntu 19 VM Image for possible future use, spotted this error in among the integration test results.  Fortunately it seems to reliably reproduce, though **note:  This VM image is experimental, it may not have the latest PPA packages since building them was recently (temporarily) disabled.**  Meaning, the environment may not represent a sane set of dependencies.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. On a F29+ system, as a regular user, having sudo access to execute podman, and GCP access to the libpod project.  From a clone of the libpod repository execute: `hack/get_ci_vm.sh ubuntu-19-libpod-6542469310709760`

2. Edit `Makefile` (on the VM), change the ginkgo target's command to: ``ginkgo -v -tags "$(BUILDTAGS)" $(GINKGOTIMEOUT) -focus "podman pod doesn't share PIDNS if requested to not"  -progress -trace -debug test/e2e/.``

3. Execute `contrib/cirrus/integration_test.sh`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
...cut...
Podman pod create 
  podman pod doesn't share PIDNS if requested to not
  /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/pod_infra_container_test.go:176
[BeforeEach] Podman pod create
  /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/pod_infra_container_test.go:21
[It] podman pod doesn't share PIDNS if requested to not
  /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/pod_infra_container_test.go:176
...cut...
Running: /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/bin/podman --storage-opt vfs.imagestore=/tmp/podman/imagecachedir --root /tmp/podman_test589871208/crio --runroot /tmp/podman_test589871208/crio-run --runtime /usr/bin/runc --conmon /usr/libexec/podman/conmon --cni-config-dir /etc/cni/net.d --cgroup-manager systemd --tmpdir /tmp/podman_test589871208 --events-backend file --storage-driver vfs top test-ctr pid
PID
1
panic: error opening "/tmp/podman_test589871208/crio/storage.lock"

goroutine 1 [running]:
...cut...
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The test should pass w/o error

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.2-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.4
  podman version: 1.6.2-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.11.3
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.1, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "19.04"
  MemFree: 2318450688
  MemTotal: 4134125568
  OCIRuntime:
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: cevich-ubuntu-19-libpod-6542469310709760
  kernel: 5.0.0-1011-gcp
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 47m 29.32s
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: {}
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 0

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

Compiled from 
95db28c7537cbf05ef8c1416f7bea35e193404e

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```
conmon-2.0.1-1~ubuntu19.04~ppa3-amd64
containernetworking-plugins-0.8.2-1~ubuntu19.04~ppa3-amd64
containers-common-0.1.39-1~ubuntu19.04~ppa3-all
cri-o-runc-1.0.0-1~rc8~ubuntu19.04~ppa1-amd64

podman-1.6.1-1~ubuntu19.04~ppa3-amd64
skopeo-0.1.39-1~ubuntu19.04~ppa3-amd64
slirp4netns-0.4.1-1~ubuntu19.04~ppa4-amd64
```

comments.body: We're throwing away the error in the panic here, which makes debugging difficult

@mheon said:

> [14:41] <mheon> the panic throws away the error message
> [14:41] <mheon> that's just lovely
> [14:41] <mheon> it's an error with unix.Open on a container file lock
> [14:42] <mheon> unix.open is just open(2)
> [14:42] <mheon> but the panic messages is throwing away the errno
> [14:42] <mheon> so i have no idea what's actually going on here
> [14:42] <mheon> could be an ENOENT
> [14:42] <mheon> could be something more serious

@mheon am I correct believing that just gives us more info. but doesn't solve the problem?

Assuming so, I'll try to reproduce this again and see what we get...
@cevich that's correct.  We no longer discard information which we hope will help with troubleshooting.
